steps.title: Find the median of each data set
steps.content:
From the dot plots, both Data Set A and Data Set B are centered at value 7.
Data Set A (20 values): cumulative count reaches the 10th and 11th values at 7, so median A = 7.
Data Set B (19 values): the 10th value falls at 7, so median B = 7.
Statement I is true: the medians are equal.

steps.title: Compare the standard deviations
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures spread around the mean.
Data Set A is sharply peaked at its center (values clustered tightly near 7).
Data Set B has a flatter, more uniform spread across the range.
A tighter cluster means smaller standard deviation; a broader spread means larger standard deviation.
The two distributions have different shapes, so their standard deviations differ.
Statement II is false.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: I and II — Statement II is false, so this is incorrect. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: I only — Statement I is true and Statement II is false. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: II only — Statement II is false. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: Neither I nor II — Statement I is true. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
